title: Avoid deeply nested control flow
description: Deeply nested control structures create "spaghetti code" that is difficult to understand, test, and maintain. Excessive nesting reduces readability and increases the cognitive load required to comprehend program logic.
recommendation: Reduce nesting by using early returns, extracting methods, or simplifying conditional logic.

noncompliant.description: Deep nesting makes the code difficult to read and understand.

compliant.description: Use early returns and extract helper functions to reduce nesting levels.
